high.claim: spec claims Linear two-way sync — webhook triggers builds AND state updates flow back to Linear
high.reality: webhook.py implements only the inbound half: signature verification and parse_linear_event() converts incoming Linear payloads into build briefs. There is no outbound issueUpdate mutation, no linear_update_issue call, and no HTTP client targeting the Linear API in any of the provided source files. The sync is one-directional (Linear → Pi-CEO builds only).
high.recommendation: Add an outbound Linear issueUpdate GraphQL mutation call in routes/webhooks.py or sessions.py (after build phase completion) to update the triggering issue state — e.g. transition to 'In Progress' on build start and 'Done'/'Cancelled' on completion — to close the sync loop.

high.claim: spec/MCP describe Telegram bot commands (/start, /help, /build) as implemented
high.reality: dashboard/app/api/telegram/route.ts contains only helper functions (send, getLinearBacklog, createLinearIssue, getPiCeoStatus, triggerBuild). The file is truncated mid-statement inside triggerBuild and no command-dispatch/routing logic is visible — there is no switch/if block that matches incoming message.text against '/start', '/help', or '/build', meaning these commands are not demonstrably wired to any handler in the provided source.
high.recommendation: Complete the POST handler with explicit command routing (e.g. if (text === '/start') ... else if (text === '/help') ... else if (text?.startsWith('/build')) ...) and confirm the file is not silently truncated in the repo.

high.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
high.reality: scripts/smoke_test.py is an external HTTP probe (urllib-based, hits live endpoints) — it is not an integration or E2E test of the build pipeline. No test file covering the session creation → generator → evaluator → push chain is present in the provided code. test_auth.py and test_webhook.py are pure unit tests of isolated helpers, not pipeline stages.
high.recommendation: Add a pytest integration test (e.g. tests/test_pipeline_e2e.py) that mocks the Claude SDK and exercises the full sessions.py phase sequence — clone, plan, generate, evaluate, push — asserting each phase transitions correctly rather than relying on an external HTTP probe.

low.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
low.reality: CLAUDE.md explicitly documents '3 pre-existing failures in test_sdk_phase2.py (claude_agent_sdk not installed locally)'; the spec presents coverage as ✅ Complete without qualifying these known failures, creating a misleading coverage signal.
low.recommendation: Add a pytest.ini [known_failures] marker or xfail decorators on the 3 sdk_phase2 tests so CI distinguishes expected-missing-dep failures from regressions, and update the spec claim to note the qualification.

low.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
low.reality: The smoke_test.py file is truncated in the provided source (ends mid-function inside _req); the actual check count cannot be verified from the code as provided. If the file is incomplete on disk the suite may run fewer than 22 checks.
low.recommendation: Verify the on-disk file is not truncated and add a self-counting assertion or a CI step that fails if the detected check count drops below 22.
